votes.question: On the Concurrent Resolution
votes.vote_description: S.Con.Res. 33, As Amended; A concurrent resolution setting forth the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2026 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2027 through 2035.

bill.ai_summary: S.Con.Res. 33 establishes the congressional budget for the U.S. government for fiscal year 2026 and outlines budgetary levels for the following years, from 2027 to 2035. This resolution affects federal spending and revenue decisions, impacting various government programs and services. It serves as a guideline for future appropriations and fiscal policy.

amendment.purpose: No Statement of Purpose on File.

votes.question: On the Amendment
votes.vote_description: Schiff Amdt. No. 5333; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to requiring the obligation of amounts appropriated to the Federal Emergency Management Agency to carry out the public assistance and hazard mitigation programs.

bill.ai_summary: The Schiff Amendment No. 5333 creates a reserve fund that ensures any money allocated to the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) for public assistance and hazard mitigation programs does not increase the federal deficit. This amendment affects FEMA's funding and its ability to respond to emergencies and implement safety measures.

amendment.purpose: To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to requiring the obligation of amounts appropriated to the Federal Emergency Management Agency to carry out the public assistance and hazard mitigation programs.

votes.question: On the Amendment
votes.vote_description: Wyden Amdt. No. 5336; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to requiring the Comptroller General of the United States to conduct a study related to economic consequences of private or confidential drug pricing agreements struck by any Federal department, agency, or office with any pharmaceutical manufacturer.

bill.ai_summary: This amendment creates a reserve fund to support a study by the Comptroller General on the economic effects of private drug pricing agreements made by federal entities with pharmaceutical companies. It aims to analyze how these agreements impact the economy. The study will inform future policy decisions regarding drug pricing.

amendment.purpose: To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to requiring the Comptroller General of the United States to conduct a study related to economic consequences of private or confidential drug pricing agreements struck by any Federal department, agency, or office with any pharmaceutical manufacturer.

votes.question: On the Motion
votes.vote_description: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Padilla Amdt. No. 4855; To create a point of order against reconciliation legislation that would provide funding to Federal agencies that have unobligated funds from previous reconciliation bills.

bill.ai_summary: This motion seeks to set a rule that would prevent certain reconciliation legislation from providing new funding to federal agencies that already have unspent funds from earlier bills. It affects federal agencies by limiting their access to additional financial resources. The goal is to ensure that existing funds are utilized before allocating new ones.

amendment.purpose: To create a point of order against reconciliation legislation that would provide funding to Federal agencies that have unobligated funds from previous reconciliation bills.

votes.question: On the Motion
votes.vote_description: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Sanders Amdt. No. 5159; To reduce the price of prescription drugs in the United States by more than 50 percent by adopting Most Favored Nation drug pricing so that the American people pay no more for prescription drugs than Europeans or Canadians.

bill.ai_summary: The bill aims to lower prescription drug prices in the United States by more than 50% by implementing a pricing model that ensures Americans pay the same prices as those in Europe and Canada. This policy affects American consumers who purchase prescription medications. It seeks to make healthcare more affordable for the general public.

amendment.purpose: To reduce the price of prescription drugs in the United States by more than 50 percent by adopting Most Favored Nation drug pricing so that the American people pay no more for prescription drugs than Europeans or Canadians.

votes.question: On the Amendment
votes.vote_description: Merkley Amdt. No. 5235; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to the impacts of hedge fund ownership of single-family homes and rent prices.

bill.ai_summary: The bill creates a reserve fund that aims to address how hedge fund ownership of single-family homes affects rent prices. It seeks to ensure that any financial measures taken will not increase the deficit. This legislation primarily impacts renters and the housing market.

amendment.purpose: To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to the impacts of hedge fund ownership of single-family homes and rent prices.

votes.question: On the Amendment
votes.vote_description: Paul Amdt. No. 5378; To reduce new budget authority for functions 150, 250, 500, and 600 in order to offset $70,000,000,000 of new spending by cutting $45,000,000,000 of foreign aid, eliminating $5,000,000,000 in refugee spending, cutting $16,000,000,000 from the Department of Education, and cutting $4,000,000,000 of National Science Foundation funding.

bill.ai_summary: The Paul Amendment No. 5378 aims to reduce new budget authority by $70 billion by cutting various funding sources. It proposes to decrease foreign aid by $45 billion, eliminate $5 billion in refugee spending, cut $16 billion from the Department of Education, and reduce National Science Foundation funding by $4 billion. This amendment affects government spending priorities, particularly in foreign aid, education, and scientific research.

amendment.purpose: To reduce new budget authority for functions 150, 250, 500, and 600 in order to offset $70,000,000,000 of new spending by cutting $45,000,000,000 of foreign aid, eliminating $5,000,000,000 in refugee spending, cutting $16,000,000,000 from the Department of Education, and cutting $4,000,000,000 of National Science Foundation funding.

votes.question: On the Motion
votes.vote_description: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Markey Amdt. No. 5001; To create a point of order against legislation that would not decrease home electricity bills.

bill.ai_summary: This motion aims to set a rule that would prevent any legislation from being considered if it does not lead to a reduction in home electricity bills. It affects lawmakers and stakeholders involved in energy policy and budgeting. The goal is to ensure that any proposed laws contribute to lowering electricity costs for consumers.

amendment.purpose: To create a point of order against legislation that would not decrease home electricity bills.

votes.question: On the Motion
votes.vote_description: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Hawley Amdt. No. 4794; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to extending the prohibition on Medicaid payments to abortion providers.

bill.ai_summary: This motion aims to set up a reserve fund that would allow for extending the ban on Medicaid payments to abortion providers without increasing the deficit. It affects Medicaid funding and abortion services. The motion seeks to bypass certain budget rules to facilitate this funding change.

amendment.purpose: To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to extending the prohibition on Medicaid payments to abortion providers.

votes.question: On the Motion
votes.vote_description: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Kennedy Amdt. No. 5414; To provide reconciliation instructions for the Committee on Rules and Administration and establish deficit-neutral reserve funds relating to establishing identification requirements for registration to vote in elections for Federal office, establishing photo identification requirements for voting in elections for Federal office, and election day and the counting of ballots in Federal elections.

amendment.purpose: To provide reconciliation instructions for the Committee on Rules and Administration and establish deficit-neutral reserve funds relating to establishing identification requirements for registration to vote in elections for Federal office, establishing photo identification requirements for voting in elections for Federal office, and election day and the counting of ballots in Federal elections.

votes.question: On the Amendment
votes.vote_description: Graham Amdt. No. 5281; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to the apprehension and deportation of adult illegal aliens convicted of rape, murder, or sexual abuse of a minor after illegally entering the United States.

This amendment primarily affects individuals who fall into these categories and the resources allocated for their enforcement. It aims to address public safety concerns related to these specific offenses.

amendment.purpose: To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to the apprehension and deportation of adult illegal aliens convicted of rape, murder, or sexual abuse of a minor after illegally entering the United States.
